content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit() (edit_file -> edit_files). Need to own these two files.

fields.note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit() (edit_file -> edit_files). Need to own these two files.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch the same 2 files (Editor class + edit() signature), so a fully disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential ownership. I'll take both files now, implement multi-file edit support (small, localized change: edit_file->edit_files, filename type, overloads), then send DONE and release. You then take ownership, pull the current file state, and layer your process_priority param on top. This avoids simultaneous clobbering. OK with you going second, or do you need to go first instead?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - you go first with process_priority change, send DONE with summary of lines touched, then I'll implement multi-file edit support on top of your changes.

fields.note: Agreed - you go first with process_priority change, send DONE with summary of lines touched, then I'll implement multi-file edit support on top of your changes.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file edit support. Summary of changes: (1) _termui_impl.py: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), joins multiple quoted filenames in the subprocess command; Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of self.edit_file(name). (2) termui.py: edit()'s filename param type is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'; body normalizes filename into a tuple (single str wrapped, iterable consumed via tuple()) and calls ed.edit_files(filenames) instead of ed.edit_file(filename). Editor.__init__ signature untouched by me. All yours now - feel free to layer process_priority on top of Editor.__init__/get_editor/edit_files and the edit() function signature.

(2) termui.py: edit()'s filename param type is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'; body normalizes filename into a tuple (single str wrapped, iterable consumed via tuple()) and calls ed.edit_files(filenames) instead of ed.edit_file(filename). Editor.__init__ signature untouched by me. All yours now - feel free to layer process_priority on top of Editor.__init__/get_editor/edit_files and the edit() function signature.
